1) Color and transparency: Golden yellow; semi-transparent with mild haze, clearer toward the meniscus and slightly duller toward the bottom.
2) Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes – faint, pale flocculent areas near the bottom/left and a diffuse clouding; no discrete solid clot or gel plug seen.

4) Density and homogeneity: Moderate optical density; mildly inhomogeneous with a basal cloudy layer/patches.

Sample B (Image 2)
1) Color and transparency: Golden yellow; semi-transparent, slightly more turbid than A.
2) Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes – a thin thread-like filament visible along the left side within the fluid and small flocculent clouds; no compact clot mass observed.
3) Coagulation stage: early_fibrin (more conspicuous strands/aggregates than A).
4) Density and homogeneity: Moderate optical density; inhomogeneous with localized strands/flocs.
5) Unique features: Distinct filament/strand and scattered cloudlets; droplets/marks on the inner wall; overall most visible fibrin-like structures among the three.

Sample C (Image 3)
1) Color and transparency: Golden yellow to light amber; predominantly transparent with minimal haze.
2) Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: No – no discernible strands, clumps, or gel-like masses.
3) Coagulation stage: none (no visible coagulation structures).
4) Density and homogeneity: Uniform optical density; visually homogeneous throughout.
5) Unique features: Smooth meniscus; broad, even illumination gradient with no internal particulates apparent.

Comparison

- Least coagulated: Sample C (clear and homogeneous).
- Visible differences: A and B both show internal clouding; B has a clear filament/strand and greater turbidity. A shows a subtle basal haze without a discrete strand. C appears uniform and clearer than both A and B. Color tones are similar across all three.
- Overall visual difference: Moderate (structure/turbidity differences are evident despite similar color).

openai.raw:
Per-sample assessments

Sample A (Image 1)
- Color and transparency: Pale lemon‑yellow core with a faint, nearly colorless halo; overall semi‑transparent with slight turbidity.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. A cohesive gelatinous plug is present; a thin membranous film is visible where the sample contacts the container wall. No long free strands seen.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (gel holds shape, thicker central mass).
- Density and homogeneity: Appears relatively dense and cohesive centrally; less material in the peripheral halo; moderately heterogeneous (distinct core vs rim).
- Unique features: Cup‑like top indentation and a circumscribing contact rim; smooth surface; small peripheral film on the glass.

Sample B (Image 2)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow central mass surrounded by a thicker, grey‑tinged translucent gel; more turbid than A.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Visible internal lamellae/striations consistent with fibrin sheets; broad gelatinous matrix surrounding the yellow core.
- Coagulation stage: full_coagulation (large, cohesive gel that maintains shape with internal fibrillar pattern).
- Density and homogeneity: High apparent density; relatively homogeneous gelatinous body with faint internal patterning.
- Unique features: Peripheral spread with smooth meniscus; fine internal streaks; granular look in the surrounding gel.

Sample C (Image 3)
- Color and transparency: Three dome‑shaped aliquots, each grey‑to‑straw translucent with a pale yellow central core; semi‑transparent.
- Clots/fibrin/coagulation structures: Yes. Each aliquot forms a stable, hemispherical gel; no free strands; small trapped bubbles/reflections on surfaces.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot to full_coagulation; based on shape retention and opacity, closer to partial_clot–full boundary. Assigning partial_clot for conservatism.
- Density and homogeneity: High cohesion; smooth, uniform domes; slightly clearer than B.
- Unique features: Consistent dome geometry; central “nucleus”-like yellow region within each dome.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample B (largest, most opaque gel with internal fibrillar pattern).
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample A (smaller mass with thinner halo and fewer internal structures).
- Visible differences: B is the most turbid and expansive with clear fibrillar/lamellar features; A is a compact plug with a thin peripheral film; C forms stable domes similar to B but appears slightly clearer and less internally structured.
- Overall visual difference: Moderate.

openai.raw:
Per-sample assessment (visual only):

Sample A (Image 1)
1) Color/transparency: Pale yellow core with a clear, nearly colorless halo; overall semi-transparent (core appears more opaque).
2) Coagulation structures: Yes. A thin whitish film/rim surrounds the drop with a few wispy thread-like strands; no large bulk clot.
3) Coagulation stage: early_fibrin (localized film, minimal network).
4) Density/homogeneity: Dense, smooth central mass; periphery much less dense; inhomogeneous (core vs rim).
5) Unique features: Lens-shaped drop adhered to tube wall; subtle surface film; isolated filament above the drop.

Sample B (Image 2)
1) Color/transparency: Yellow central deposit; broad peripheral film with gray‑green/yellow tint; semi-transparent film, more opaque center.
2) Coagulation structures: Yes. Extensive fibrin sheet with polygonal, crack-like network and fine radial striations; scattered dot-like inclusions.
3) Coagulation stage: partial_clot (continuous networked film spanning area, not as thick as C).
4) Density/homogeneity: Markedly heterogeneous; denser near center with variable film thickness and cracking.
5) Unique features: Wide honeycomb/cracked pattern, starburst lines, dotted inclusions in the film.

Sample C (Image 3)
1) Color/transparency: Stronger yellow overall; central mass yellow and opaque; surrounding yellow-tinged film semi-transparent.
2) Coagulation structures: Yes. Robust, continuous fibrin network with pronounced polygonal “honeycomb” regions and a thick annular rim.
3) Coagulation stage: full_coagulation (thick, cohesive network and rim around the deposit).
4) Density/homogeneity: Highest apparent density; network more continuous and organized than B; still regionally heterogeneous.
5) Unique features: Prominent yellow rim with rounded compartments; extensive uniform polygonal lattice; fine micro-texture on one side.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample C.
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample A.
- Visible differences: Progression from minimal rim/threads (A) to broad cracked fibrin sheet (B) to thicker, more continuous honeycomb network with a pronounced rim (C). Color intensity also increases from A to C, with B showing a cooler gray-green tint in the film.
- Overall visual difference: Pronounced.

Sample A (Image 1)

Sample A (Image 1)

openai.raw:
Independent assessments

Sample A (Image 1)
- Color and transparency: Pale yellow; overall semi-transparent with locally opaque regions.
- Clots/fibrin: Yes. A large, cohesive gelatinous mass adheres to the tube wall; visible folds and lamellae.
- Coagulation stage: full_coagulation.
- Density/homogeneity: Dense gel; heterogeneous thickness; very little free liquid observed.
- Unique features: Disc/sheet-like coagulum with smooth edges and internal striations.

Sample B (Image 2)
- Color and transparency: Bright yellow; transparent.
- Clots/fibrin: Yes. Thin, wispy veil/film along the lower wall; scant strands; bulk of the volume remains fluid.
- Coagulation stage: early_fibrin.
- Density/homogeneity: Mostly uniform low-viscosity liquid; localized nonuniformity where the veil is attached.
- Unique features: Curved, curtain-like fibrin film near the bottom; no discrete solid clot.

Sample C (Image 3)
- Color and transparency: Bright yellow; transparent.
- Clots/fibrin: Yes. More extensive thin sheets/lamellae covering much of the bottom and lower side; thicker than in Sample B.
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot.
- Density/homogeneity: Predominantly clear fluid with a broader adherent fibrin sheet; not a solid mass.
- Unique features: Network-like sheet with multiple folds crossing the bottom hemisphere.

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Sample A.
- Least advanced coagulation: Sample B.
- Visible differences: A contains a bulky, semi-opaque coagulum occupying a large area with minimal free liquid; B is mostly clear fluid with only a delicate fibrin veil; C is intermediate, showing broader fibrin sheets than B but lacking A’s solid gel. A appears paler and less clear than the bright, transparent B and C.
- Overall visual difference: pronounced.
